etymology.description: 世(世) (shì, "generation; lifetime") and 界(界) (jiè, "boundary; realm") combine to form 世界(世界), which refers to the "world" as the realm or entire space where generations of people live.
etymology.interpretation: 世界(世界) (shìjiè, "world") combines the concept of 世(世) (shì, "generation; lifetime") with 界(界) (jiè, "boundary; realm") to describe the totality of human civilization across time and space. It conveys the idea of the human world or the earth as the domain for generations of people.

examples.chinese: 他想环游世界(他想環遊世界)。
examples.english: He wants to travel around the world.

examples.chinese: 世界上有很多美丽的地方(世界上有很多美麗的地方)。
examples.pinyin: Shìjiè shàng yǒu hěn duō měilì de dìfāng.
examples.english: There are many beautiful places in the world.
